msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 130 is another attachment-only continuation. I used only the three files attached in this message: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md; I did not use File Library material.

Turn 130 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The target remains exactly the same: decide whether the profinite one-relator quotients ΓS,ΓH are isomorphic over the fixed quotient G, equivalently whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH withπH∘Φ=πS.This is the condition stated in the attached prompt and problem statement.The current two-coordinate state remains unchanged from Turn 129:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached history records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement:∃Φ:F∼

F over GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G condition.No structural subcase should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history’s uniform reason is still the same: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate is also unchanged. The current attachments contain only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts: no independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 130 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 129. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN130-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
